scenario_name: Spotify Joe Rogan COVID Misinformation Controversy 2022
description: Neil Young and other artists removed music from Spotify in protest of Joe Rogan's COVID misinformation podcasts. Sparked debate about platform responsibility. Spotify lost ~1.5M subscribers temporarily but retained Rogan. Tests platform content moderation crisis with divided audience.

key_events.description: Neil Young issues ultimatum to Spotify: remove Joe Rogan's podcast or remove his music. Spotify removes Young's catalog. #CancelSpotify trends.
Joni Mitchell, Nils Lofgren, and 270 medical professionals join boycott. Compilation of Rogan using racial slurs surfaces, compounding crisis.
Spotify CEO Daniel Ek announces $100M investment in diverse audio content and adds content advisories to COVID episodes. Rogan apologizes for racial slur clips.
Spotify reports Q4 2021 earnings showing 1.5M subscriber loss attributed partially to controversy. Stock drops 17% (though broader market factors involved).
Controversy fades from news cycle. Subscriber numbers recover. Rogan remains Spotify's most popular podcast. Boycott loses momentum.

calibration_notes: Tests platform content moderation crisis with fading boycott dynamics. Unlike a product safety crisis, this was an identity/values conflict where the platform's audience was split. Key dynamics: boycott momentum peaking then fading (typical 2-3 week attention cycle), artist solidarity as novel pressure mechanism, platform choosing revenue over reputation, racial slur revelation as compounding secondary crisis, apology and content advisory as partial de-escalation, and the structural difficulty of sustained consumer boycotts against subscription platforms with network effects.
